codeReview.summary: The legacy RSA repair/reuse flow is covered, but the new runtime PEM loader still accepts a narrower set of PEM keys than the import/repair path, so some saved encrypted PEM identities can import successfully and then fail in the real session flow.

issues.description: `SshSessionCoordinator.loadKeyPair()` only routes saved keys through the new BouncyCastle PEM loader when `looksLikePemPrivateKey()` matches `BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY`, `BEGIN PRIVATE KEY`, or `BEGIN ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY` (`core/ssh/src/main/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/core/ssh/SshSessionCoordinator.kt:1083-1086`). The paired import/repair path now recognizes additional PEM forms including `BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY` and `BEGIN DSA PRIVATE KEY` (`feature/identities/src/main/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/feature/identities/ImportedKeyImportService.kt:242-246`). That means an encrypted EC/DSA PEM can import and persist successfully, but the saved-host/session path will skip the PEM loader and fall back to MINA's generic parser at runtime, reintroducing the import/runtime mismatch this feature was supposed to close and breaking host/session truthfulness for supported encrypted PEM identities outside the specific RSA fixture exercised by the new test.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Imported-key PEM parsing is duplicated between the identity import service and the SSH runtime without a documented shared contract, and the accepted header sets have already drifted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `feature/identities/src/main/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/feature/identities/ImportedKeyImportService.kt:242-246` matches RSA/EC/DSA/PKCS#8 PEM headers, while `core/ssh/src/main/kotlin/io/github/jtsang4/aterm/core/ssh/SshSessionCoordinator.kt:1083-1086` only matches RSA/PKCS#8 headers.

summary: Fail: the reviewed commit fixes the legacy RSA blocked→repair→reuse fixture path, but the new session-side PEM detection still accepts a narrower set of PEM keys than the import path, so some imported encrypted PEM identities can still look valid in the library yet fail in saved-host session flows.
